claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you claiming Child Benefit for, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have one child to assess - Alex.

Before I check the eligibility rules, I need to gather a bit more information about Alex's circumstances.

**How old is Alex, or what is his date of birth?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to check the eligibility rules to give you accurate information. Let me look up the specific requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the eligibility rules I've just reviewed, I can see that Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over has specific requirements. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask you a few more questions to determine his eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as full-time school, college, university, or an approved apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training. He's working full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask one more question to complete the assessment:

**How many hours per week is Alex working in his full-time job?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's working full-time - that's over 24 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the necessary information. Let me now assess Alex's eligibility based on the Child Benefit rules.

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 18 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He is working full-time (over 24 hours per week)

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you will **stop getting Child Benefit** if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training.

Since Alex meets both of these conditions, he is no longer eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

The reason is that Alex is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit stops when a child reaches this situation, even if they are under 20 years old.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future - for example, if he returns to approved education or training - you may be able to reclaim Child Benefit at that point. If you have any questions or if his situation does change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and working full-time (over 24 hours per week). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in approved education or training and is working over 24 hours per week, he does not qualify for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) and is no longer in approved education or training, which makes him ineligible under the Child Benefit rules."}

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and working full-time (over 24 hours per week). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in approved education or training and is working over 24 hours per week, he does not qualify for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) and is no longer in approved education or training, which makes him ineligible under the Child Benefit rules."}
